title: IN-2: Force Layout Integration
description:
Wire force layout to live artifacts (not just mock data).
- When new cards are created via tool calls, add them to the force simulation
- Semantic edges derived from tags/placement hints
- Cards cluster after creation
- Ghost cards excluded until solidified (300ms delay)

Acceptance: Speak and create 5 cards. Cards settle into semantic clusters via force layout. Related cards (shared tags) cluster together.
acceptance_criteria: Speak and create 5 cards. Cards settle into semantic clusters via force layout. Related cards (shared tags) cluster together.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Emit explicit layout-settled signals/signatures that downstream beads can consume for topology analysis, synthesis triggering, snapshot recording, and replay fidelity.
- Preserve stable artifact identity while the DOM is manipulated directly during ticks so drag/replay/test selectors do not churn.
- Reheat and settle behavior should be deterministic enough that users can trust the semantic clustering rather than reading it as jitter.

title: IN-6: Replay Integration
description:
Wire live state snapshots to the replay UI.
- Every tool call, drag, and layout settle creates a snapshot
- Replay scrubber shows actual session history
- Playing back compresses session into time-lapse

Acceptance: After a full interaction (5+ cards, research, calendar), hit replay. 10-second time-lapse shows entire session evolution.
acceptance_criteria: After a full interaction (5+ cards, research, calendar), hit replay. 10-second time-lapse shows entire session evolution.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- The replay target is the full golden-path session, not just card creation. Final acceptance should include synthesis cards, job nodes, research-result cards, calendar confirmation cards, and any created edges when those events exist in the source session.

title: BE-4: move_artifact Handler
description:
Implement the move_artifact tool per spec §7.2.
- Parameters: artifact_id, placement, near_artifact_id, x, y
- Calculate new position from placement hint
- Update artifact position in Firestore
- Return { artifact_id, status: moved, position: { x, y } }
- Notify frontend

Acceptance: Gemini calls move_artifact → position updates in Firestore → frontend moves card.
acceptance_criteria: Gemini calls move_artifact → position updates in Firestore → frontend moves card.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Cover the full spec placement enum set: near_artifact, viewport_center, viewport_left, viewport_right, viewport_top, viewport_bottom, and coordinates.
- Log placement-resolution decisions and clamp/validate coordinate inputs so invalid tool arguments fail clearly.

title: BE-8: Topology Analysis
description:
Compute cluster density and isolation after force layout settles. Per spec §8.6.
- Implement computeTopology(artifacts) from spec §8.6 code example
- Tight cluster: cards within 100px
- Isolate: card >400px from nearest neighbor
- Format as [TOPOLOGY_ANALYSIS] text (spec §8.6 format)
- Inject via sendClientContent() after force layout settle
- Only inject if topology changed since last injection
- Minimum interval: 5 seconds

Acceptance: With 5+ cards on canvas after force layout settles, topology analysis is computed and injected. Agent receives and can reference cluster insights in speech.
acceptance_criteria: With 5+ cards on canvas after force layout settles, topology analysis is computed and injected. Agent receives and can reference cluster insights in speech.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Include loose-cluster detection from the spec, not just tight clusters and isolates.
- Compute and compare a topology signature/hash so injections are based on meaningful structural change, not merely rerunning the same analysis.
- Run topology analysis only after the live layout has actually settled or the freeze timeout has elapsed.

title: DY-2: Deploy Script
description:
Create scripts/deploy.sh for bonus points (+0.2).
- Automated deployment script per spec §2.4
- Sets env vars, runs gcloud deploy command
- Include in repo root

Acceptance: ./scripts/deploy.sh deploys the app to Cloud Run successfully.
acceptance_criteria: ./scripts/deploy.sh deploys the app to Cloud Run successfully.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- The deploy script should codify the exact Cloud Run flags/env wiring from the final deployment bead and be safe to rerun.
- It should surface missing required environment variables loudly instead of silently deploying a broken service.

title: DM-3: Submit to Devpost
description:
Complete and submit the Devpost entry.
- Select category: Live Agents
- Text description (DL-3)
- Public GitHub repo URL
- Demo video YouTube URL (DM-1)
- Cloud proof link/upload (DM-2)
- Architecture diagram upload (DL-1)
- Blog post URL (DL-2)

Acceptance: Submission is complete on Devpost. All required fields filled. All links public and working.
acceptance_criteria: Submission is complete on Devpost. All required fields filled. All links public and working.

PLAN REVISION:
- The final submission bead should carry the pre-submit checklist from spec §14: public YouTube, public GitHub, deployed URL loads, all Devpost fields filled, testing instructions present, and submission complete before the 4:00 PM PDT buffer.

title: IN-1: End-to-End Audio + Tool Wiring
description:
Wire everything together for the core loop: speak → Gemini → tool calls → cards on canvas.
- Browser audio capture → WebSocket → backend → Gemini Live API
- Gemini tool calls → tool executor → Firestore → frontend notification
- Frontend receives notification → renders ghost card → solidifies
- Gemini audio response → backend → browser → playback
- System prompt loaded, agent responds as Eureka persona
- Verify: speaking about a project creates cards SILENTLY (no agent narration during creation)

Acceptance: Speak naturally about 3 project concerns. 3+ ghost cards appear silently during speech. Cards solidify. After pause, agent speaks one brief insight. No card creation narration.
acceptance_criteria: Speak naturally about 3 project concerns. 3+ ghost cards appear silently during speech. Cards solidify. After pause, agent speaks one brief insight. No card creation narration.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Treat the core loop as a trust-building experience, not just plumbing: the connection indicator, transcript fallback, and controls must all be present so a user can understand what the system is doing while testing the live path.
- Instrument the silence requirement directly by logging whether any output audio was emitted during card-creation windows.

title: CU-14: Research Job Streaming Progress
description:
Track the spec’s P2 streaming-progress enhancement separately from the baseline job-node work.

SCOPE:
- Extend the research job node so it can show staged progress updates while the research runner is active.
- Progress should communicate phases such as queued, searching, synthesizing, and writing findings.
- The UI must remain compatible with the simpler running/complete/failed job-node states from the baseline build.
- Progress updates should be optional and degrade cleanly to the simpler pulsing-running state.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. A running research job can display stage-based progress updates without replacing the baseline job-node UI.
2. Progress updates stream in order and stop once the job completes or fails.
3. Completion/failure states override the progress indicator cleanly.
4. Replay can represent the progress transitions if snapshot granularity allows.
5. The feature degrades to the simpler running pulse when streaming progress is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A running research job can display stage-based progress updates without replacing the baseline job-node UI.

title: IN-7: Workspace Restore + Reload Recovery
description:
Implement the save/restore experience required by spec §§4.3, 9, and 11.3 so a refresh or revisit does not destroy the user’s thinking space.

SCOPE:
- Establish a durable workspace-identity flow for local/demo use (query param, stored ID, or equivalent deterministic bootstrap).
- On initial load, fetch the workspace plus persisted artifacts, edges, jobs, and snapshots from Firestore.
- Hydrate the live canvas with heterogeneous artifact types, not just plain cards: synthesis cards, research job nodes, research-result cards, calendar confirmation cards, and edges.
- Rehydrate replay history from persisted snapshots when available so replay survives refreshes.
- Reconcile hydrated state with the live WebSocket/session startup sequence so a reconnect does not duplicate artifacts or briefly flash an empty canvas.
- Handle empty workspaces, partially written records, and stale/malformed documents gracefully with structured diagnostics.
- Emit structured logs for hydration timings, document counts, dropped records, and restore success/failure.

USER VALUE:
- Users can reload or reopen the workspace without losing the visual structure the agent created with them.
- Replay remains a trustworthy record of the session rather than an in-memory-only effect.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After a golden-path session, reloading the app restores the current artifacts, edges, and heterogeneous artifact visuals from Firestore.
2. Persisted replay snapshots rehydrate into the replay UI when snapshot data exists.
3. A brand-new or empty workspace loads a clean canvas without errors.
4. Partial or malformed persisted data is skipped or surfaced with actionable logs instead of crashing the app.
5. The restored workspace is immediately usable for continued live interaction, not read-only.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Treat persistence as a full user experience, not just CRUD plumbing: the first frame after refresh should already reflect the user’s prior thinking state.
- Preserve stable artifact IDs and edge IDs through hydration so replay interpolation, DOM hooks, and future tool calls remain coherent.
- If replay fidelity requires optional viewport/edge metadata beyond the base spec fields, store it alongside snapshots without weakening the required schema.

title: BE-14: delete_artifact Tool + Single-Level Undo
description:
Capture the spec’s deferred deletion/undo capability without making it part of the demo-critical path.

SCOPE:
- Implement a delete_artifact tool that removes an artifact and any invalidated relationships safely.
- Add a single-level undo path so the most recent delete can be reversed.
- Decide explicitly whether deletion is soft-delete/tombstone or hard-delete, but preserve replay/history integrity either way.
- Ensure snapshots and canvas replay can represent the delete and undo transitions.
- Prevent accidental cascading corruption when deleting grouped, linked, or replayed artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. delete_artifact removes the targeted artifact and updates dependent edges/state consistently.
2. The most recent delete can be undone once.
3. Replay/history remain coherent after delete and undo operations.
4. Duplicate undo or invalid delete requests fail safely with clear errors/logs.
5. The baseline build remains unaffected when this upgrade is not implemented.
acceptance_criteria: 1. delete_artifact removes the targeted artifact and updates dependent edges/state consistently.

title: TS-1: Audio Pipeline Unit Tests
description:
Comprehensive unit tests for the entire audio pipeline with detailed logging.
Test framework: Vitest. File: __tests__/audio-pipeline.test.ts

=== TEST SUITE 1: AudioWorklet Processor (AU-1) ===

Test 1.1: Downsample 48kHz → 16kHz
- Input: 480 Float32 samples at 48kHz (10ms of audio)
- Expected: 160 Int16 samples at 16kHz
- Verify: output length = input length * (16000/48000)
- Verify: values are correctly averaged across 3-sample windows (48k/16k = 3)
- Log: input sample count, output sample count, ratio, first 10 output values

Test 1.2: Downsample 44100Hz → 16kHz
- Input: 441 Float32 samples at 44.1kHz (10ms of audio)
- Expected: 160 Int16 samples at 16kHz (with interpolation)
- Verify: output length is correct
- Verify: no aliasing artifacts (output values within expected range)
- Log: interpolation points used, output sample count

Test 1.3: Float32 → Int16 conversion
- Input: [0.0, 1.0, -1.0, 0.5, -0.5, 0.999969] as Float32
- Expected: [0, 32767, -32768, 16383, -16384, 32767] as Int16
- Verify: clamping at boundaries (values > 1.0 clamp to 32767, < -1.0 clamp to -32768)
- Log: each input → output pair

Test 1.4: Empty input handling
- Input: empty Float32Array
- Expected: empty Int16Array, no errors
- Log: confirmed graceful empty handling

Test 1.5: Chunk size consistency
- Process 100 consecutive 10ms chunks
- Verify: total output samples = expected for 1 second at 16kHz (16000)
- Log: per-chunk sizes, cumulative total

=== TEST SUITE 2: Audio Playback Resampling (AU-4) ===

Test 2.1: Resample 24kHz → 48kHz
- Input: 240 Int16 samples at 24kHz (10ms)
- Expected: 480 Float32 samples at 48kHz (2x upsample)
- Verify: linear interpolation between samples
- Log: input/output lengths, first 10 interpolated values

Test 2.2: Resample 24kHz → 44100Hz
- Input: 240 Int16 samples at 24kHz (10ms)
- Expected: 441 Float32 samples at 44100Hz (1.8375x upsample with interpolation)
- Verify: output length correct, values properly interpolated
- Log: ratio, interpolation details

Test 2.3: Int16 → Float32 conversion for playback
- Input: [0, 32767, -32768, 16384] as Int16
- Expected: [0.0, ~1.0, ~-1.0, ~0.5] as Float32
- Verify: normalized to [-1.0, 1.0] range
- Log: each conversion

Test 2.4: Buffer queuing
- Queue 5 audio chunks rapidly
- Verify: chunks play sequentially without gaps or overlap
- Verify: queue length tracking is accurate
- Log: queue state after each enqueue, play timing

Test 2.5: Interruption handling
- Queue 3 chunks, then trigger interruption
- Verify: playback stops immediately
- Verify: queue is cleared
- Verify: pending chunks are discarded
- Log: queue state before/after interruption, timing

=== TEST SUITE 3: AudioCapture Wrapper ===

Test 3.1: Start/stop lifecycle
- Mock AudioContext and AudioWorklet
- Call start() → verify AudioContext created, mic requested, processor registered
- Call stop() → verify tracks stopped, context closed
- Log: lifecycle events with timestamps

Test 3.2: onChunk callback
- Mock processor posting messages
- Verify: onChunk fires with correct ArrayBuffer data
- Log: chunk sizes, callback count

=== LOGGING REQUIREMENTS ===
Every test must:
1. Log test name and description at start
2. Log input data summary (type, length, first few values)
3. Log expected vs actual output
4. Log PASS/FAIL with specific assertion details
5. On failure: log full input/output for debugging

Use console.log with structured format:
  [TS-1] [Suite.Test] Description | input: ... | expected: ... | actual: ... | PASS/FAIL
acceptance_criteria:
1. The Bun/Vitest audio suite covers capture DSP, playback resampling, queueing, interruption flush, and AudioCapture lifecycle behavior.
2. Tests validate both 48kHz and 44.1kHz capture inputs plus 24kHz playback resampling to common system rates.
3. Structured logs expose enough numeric detail to debug sample-count or interpolation regressions quickly.
4. Pure DSP helpers and the AudioWorklet-facing implementation stay in verified parity.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Add parity tests between the pure DSP helpers and the AudioWorklet-facing implementation so audio math cannot silently diverge between runtime and tests.
- Include queue-depth/interruption assertions for the playback path, not just sample conversion math.

title: DY-1: Cloud Run Deployment
description:
Deploy to Cloud Run per spec §5.6.
- Create Dockerfile (or use buildpacks)
- gcloud run deploy eureka-canvas --source . --region us-central1 --allow-unauthenticated --timeout=3600 --min-instances=1
- Set env vars for Gemini API key, model name, GCP project
- Verify WebSocket works on deployed instance

Acceptance: App is accessible at Cloud Run URL. Audio pipeline works on deployed version.
acceptance_criteria: App is accessible at Cloud Run URL. Audio pipeline works on deployed version.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Treat this as the final demo-ready deployment bead, not an early spike deploy.
- Deployment should happen only after the full effective-P0 integration set and local e2e verification are green.
- Verify the deployed service keeps WebSocket sessions alive with --timeout=3600 and avoids cold-start surprises with --min-instances=1.
- Capture the exact deployed URL and environment shape needed for the smoke-test and demo-recording beads.

title: DL-1: Architecture Diagram
description:
Create polished architecture diagram per spec §13.2 Shot 6.
- SVG or PNG, color-coded
- Shows: Browser → Cloud Run → Gemini Live API → Firestore → Calendar → Search grounding
- Label SDK features: Proactive Audio, FunctionResponseScheduling, thinking budget, session resumption
- Save to public/architecture.png

Acceptance: Clean, professional diagram that a judge can understand in 5 seconds. All SDK features labeled.
acceptance_criteria: Clean, professional diagram that a judge can understand in 5 seconds. All SDK features labeled.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- The diagram should reflect the final architecture decisions: server proxy, blocking tool execution in the baseline build, separate generateContent research call with Google Search grounding, Firestore persistence, Calendar API, session resumption/sliding-window compression, and on-demand snapshot grounding as an upgrade path.
- Label the exact SDK concepts judges care about without implying features that are still backlog-only.

title: BE-15: create_dag Tool + Task DAG Rendering
description:
Preserve the spec’s P2 task-DAG capability as an explicitly tracked upgrade instead of letting it disappear behind generic edge rendering.

SCOPE:
- Implement the documented create_dag tool contract from spec §7.2.
- Create dag_node artifacts plus the corresponding directed edges.
- Render the resulting graph in a readable task-DAG layout that is visually distinct from loose semantic card clustering.
- Ensure DAG nodes/edges persist, replay, and coexist with the rest of the canvas model.
- Validate edge references and prevent invalid cycles introduced by malformed tool input.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. create_dag can generate multiple nodes and directed edges from a single tool call.
2. DAG nodes render with readable structure instead of collapsing into generic overlapping cards.
3. Directed relationships remain stable through reload and replay.
4. Invalid node indexes or malformed edge definitions fail with clear errors/logs.
5. The baseline demo path remains unaffected when this upgrade is not yet implemented.
acceptance_criteria: 1. create_dag can generate multiple nodes and directed edges from a single tool call.

title: BE-1: Firestore CRUD
description:
Workspace and artifact persistence per spec §9.
- lib/firestore/ module
- firebase-admin SDK initialization
- Functions: createWorkspace(), getWorkspace(), createArtifact(), updateArtifact(), getArtifacts(), createEdge(), getEdges()
- Data models match spec §9 exactly
- Timestamps as Firestore server timestamps
- Convert Firestore Timestamps to ISO strings before returning to tool responses (spec §7.1 note about non-serializable values)

Acceptance: Can create a workspace, add artifacts, retrieve them. Firestore console shows correct data structure.
acceptance_criteria: Can create a workspace, add artifacts, retrieve them. Firestore console shows correct data structure.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Expand the persistence layer to cover the full spec data model: Workspace, Artifact, Edge, Job, and StateSnapshot CRUD/helpers.
- State snapshots may store optional replay-fidelity metadata such as edges and viewport state in addition to the required artifact array, as long as the spec-required fields remain present.
- Provide serialization helpers that strip/convert Firestore-specific types before anything reaches the live-session tool-response path.

title: BE-6: Canvas State Injection
description:
Format and inject [CANVAS_STATE] into Live API session per spec §8.2 and §8.3.
- Function: injectCanvasState(session, artifacts, edges)
- Format: spec §8.2 text format
- Injection triggers per spec §8.3: after each tool call, after user drag, after layout settle, every 30s if changed
- Use session.sendClientContent() with role: user (spec §8.4 code example)
- Track last-injected state to avoid redundant injections

Acceptance: After a tool call, canvas state is injected into the session. Format matches spec §8.2.
acceptance_criteria: After a tool call, canvas state is injected into the session. Format matches spec §8.2.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Include the adaptive trigger that happens immediately before spatial-command handling, not just after writes/settles/idle intervals.
- Encode viewport center, zoom, and visible-area metadata exactly as the spec requires so spatial references stay grounded in the shared coordinate system.
- Keep the schedule adaptive rather than reverting to a wasteful fixed interval; unchanged state should not consume context budget.

title: CU-7: Canvas Replay UI
description:
Timeline scrubber for state snapshot playback per spec §11.3.
- CanvasReplay.tsx component
- Timeline scrubber at bottom of canvas
- Accepts array of { timestamp, artifacts } snapshots
- Dragging scrubber interpolates card positions between snapshots
- Cards that dont exist at a given timestamp fade in/out
- Play button for auto-playback at configurable speed (fast for demo: 10 seconds for full session)
- Speed controls (1x, 2x, 5x, 10x)

Acceptance: Given 10 mock snapshots, scrubber plays through them. Cards appear/disappear/move smoothly. 10-second full replay works.
acceptance_criteria: Given 10 mock snapshots, scrubber plays through them. Cards appear/disappear/move smoothly. 10-second full replay works.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Replay UI should be ready for heterogeneous artifact types, not only plain cards: synthesis cards, job nodes, research-result cards, calendar confirmation cards, and edges when replay data contains them.
- Preserve artifact identity across scrubber movement so DOM/test hooks and interpolated motion remain stable.

title: BN-1: GDG Membership Signup
description:
HUMAN-ONLY TASK: Sign up for Google Developer Groups (GDG) for +0.2 bonus points.

STEPS:
1. Go to https://developers.google.com/community/gdg
2. Create/update GDG profile
3. Copy profile URL
4. Add profile URL to Devpost submission (DM-3)

Per spec §2.4: +0.2 bonus points for active GDG profile linked in submission.
On a 5-point scale, total bonus (blog + deploy script + GDG) = 1.0 = 20% boost.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An active GDG profile exists and its public URL has been captured.
2. The URL is stored where DM-3 can include it without re-discovery.
3. The signup/profile task is complete early enough that it does not jeopardize submission timing.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Treat this as a submission-readiness bead, not an optional afterthought; the URL needs to be ready before the final Devpost pass.
- Capture the exact public profile URL in the issue/thread context so the human submitter does not have to search for it under deadline pressure.

title: CU-5: SVG Edge Rendering
description:
Dependency arrows between cards per spec §8.4 and §9 (Edge data model).
- DagEdge.tsx component
- SVG overlay on canvas
- Draw arrow from source card center to target card center
- Arrow style: subtle, semi-transparent, with small arrowhead
- Update positions when cards move (force layout or drag)
- Edge data: { from_artifact_id, to_artifact_id, type }

Acceptance: Given two cards and an edge between them, an SVG arrow renders connecting them. Arrow updates when cards are repositioned.
acceptance_criteria: Given two cards and an edge between them, an SVG arrow renders connecting them. Arrow updates when cards are repositioned.

title: TS-4: E2E Integration Test Script
acceptance_criteria:
1. `scripts/e2e-test.sh` runs via Bun-only commands, executes unit suites first, then launches browser-driven end-to-end coverage.
2. The harness supports deterministic mock mode and live mode.
3. Scenarios cover the golden path plus reload/restore recovery and connection-loss/session-resumption behavior.
4. Every run emits structured per-step logs and retains timestamped artifacts such as traces, screenshots, or videos for failures.
5. The final report is concise enough to use as a pre-demo confidence gate.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Use a real browser-automation harness (Playwright or equivalent) for DOM/CSS/drag/replay verification rather than relying only on headless Vitest integration.
- Support at least two execution modes: deterministic mock mode for CI/local confidence and live mode for final human-verified smoke runs.
- The shell script should run unit suites first, then browser e2e, and persist logs/traces/screenshots/videos for failures.
- Add a reconnect/connection-loss scenario so the connection-status flow is exercised before demo recording.
- Add a refresh/reload restore scenario that verifies persisted workspace and replay state survive a page restart.
- Retain a terse scenario summary plus pointers to captured artifacts so failures are diagnosable under time pressure.

title: TS-5: Deployed Smoke Test Script
description:
Add a post-deployment smoke-test script so the team can validate the Cloud Run build before recording the demo or submitting.

FILES:
- scripts/smoke-deploy.sh
- any supporting test harness/config needed for scripted checks

SCOPE:
- Accept the deployed base URL as input and log every step with timestamps.
- Verify health endpoint reachability, app HTML load, and WebSocket handshake success.
- Check visible readiness signals that matter for judges: connection indicator state, transcript fallback presence, and replay entry point if enabled.
- Support a lightweight live/manual checkpoint section for audio/calendar/research features that cannot be fully automated safely against production.
- Write logs to a timestamped artifact so failures are diagnosable after the fact.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Running the script against a deployed URL produces a timestamped smoke-test log.
2. The script fails fast when the health endpoint, app shell, or WebSocket handshake is broken.
3. The script records enough detail to distinguish app failure from environment/config failure.
4. The output is concise enough to use before demo recording and Devpost submission.
5. The script becomes part of the final pre-submit verification path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Running the script against a deployed URL produces a timestamped smoke-test log.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Include a quick refresh/restore check after the initial page load so deployed persistence is exercised, not just the first HTML render.
- If the deployed environment supports it safely, exercise a lightweight reconnect path and log whether the indicator returns to Connected cleanly.
- Persist timestamped logs plus at least one screenshot/HTML artifact so remote failures can be diagnosed after the run.

title: BE-10: State Snapshot Recording
description:
Record canvas state snapshots for replay per spec §11.3.
- On every tool call completion, user drag, or layout settle:
  - Deep clone current artifacts array
  - Push { timestamp, artifacts, trigger } to replay buffer (in-memory array)
  - Also write to Firestore snapshots subcollection
- Expose getSnapshots() for frontend replay

Acceptance: After a sequence of tool calls and drags, snapshot array contains ordered state history. Snapshots are in Firestore.
acceptance_criteria: After a sequence of tool calls and drags, snapshot array contains ordered state history. Snapshots are in Firestore.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Persist enough replay fidelity for restore/replay trust: artifacts are mandatory, and edges/viewport metadata should be stored when available so the timeline reflects structural changes accurately.
- Snapshot recording must remain cheap and deterministic because it runs on tool completions, drag events, and layout settles.
- Emit structured logs for trigger source, artifact/edge counts, write latency, and snapshot IDs.

title: AU-1: AudioWorklet Capture Processor
acceptance_criteria:
1. AudioWorklet processor file exists at public/audio-processor.js (vanilla JS)
2. Calling start() requests mic permission, creates AudioContext, registers processor
3. onChunk fires with ArrayBuffer containing Int16 PCM at 16kHz
4. Chunk sizes consistent (~1600 samples per 100ms chunk)
5. Downsampling handles both 48kHz and 44100Hz input rates
6. Float32 → Int16 conversion clamps correctly at boundaries
7. stop() releases mic, closes context
8. Tab visibility handler resumes AudioContext on re-focus
9. Console log confirms: "AudioCapture started, native rate: {rate}Hz, target: 16000Hz"
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Avoid Array.push(...input) on long-lived audio streams; use a typed-array accumulator or ring-buffer approach to reduce GC pressure and audio dropouts.
- Mirror the DSP logic in testable pure utilities so AU-1/TS-1 can validate the exact math outside the AudioWorklet runtime.
- Normalize to mono explicitly if the browser provides multiple channels.
- Log native sample rate, chunk duration, output sample count, and dropped/late chunk counters for debugging real hardware issues.

title: AU-3: Gemini Live API Session
description:
Connect the backend to Gemini Live API per spec §5.2.
- Use @google/genai SDK
- apiVersion: v1alpha
- Full session config from spec §5.2 (system prompt, tools as BLOCKING per AGENTS.md Rule 3, proactiveAudio, thinkingConfig, transcription, sessionResumption, slidingWindow, speechConfig with Kore voice, silenceDurationMs: 700)
- VERIFY MODEL NAME gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-12-2025 resolves. If not, check docs and update.
- Tool declarations for: create_card, move_artifact, update_artifact, create_calendar_event, start_research_job (all BLOCKING — no behavior: NON_BLOCKING, per AGENTS.md Rule 3)
- Session event handlers: toolCall, audioData, interrupted, turnComplete, inputTranscription, outputTranscription
- Session resumption on disconnect

Acceptance: Backend successfully opens Live API WebSocket. Session is created with all config. Tool declarations are accepted. console.log confirms session is active.
acceptance_criteria: Backend successfully opens Live API WebSocket. Session is created with all config. Tool declarations are accepted. console.log confirms session is active.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- This bead must embed the full Eureka persona/system-prompt behavior from spec §§5.2 and 6, including: never narrate tool calls, auto-crystallize 3-5 cards per thought burst, topology-aware insight, synthesis behavior, spatial-awareness behavior, research and calendar behavior, and interrupt handling.
- Add feature flags/runtime switches for proactiveAudio, silenceDurationMs, and any future NON_BLOCKING experiment so the team can fall back quickly without rewriting the session layer.
- Verify the model name before other live-session beads proceed and log the exact model that resolved.
- Record the fallback decision matrix explicitly: blocking tools are the default build, and any future NON_BLOCKING + SILENT trial is an upgrade experiment with comparative logs.

title: AU-4: Audio Proxy Pipeline
description:
Wire browser audio → backend → Gemini and Gemini → backend → browser. Per spec §5.3.
- Browser sends 16kHz Int16 PCM chunks via WebSocket to backend
- Backend forwards to Gemini as realtimeInput with mimeType: audio/pcm;rate=16000
- Gemini returns 24kHz Int16 PCM audio
- Backend proxies audio chunks to browser via WebSocket
- Browser playback: AudioContext at system default rate, resample 24kHz→system rate, queue and play via AudioBufferSourceNode
- Interruption: when user starts speaking, stop playback, clear queue

Acceptance: Speak into mic → Gemini responds → audio plays back through speakers. Interruption stops playback. Verify round-trip works with a simple Hello test.
acceptance_criteria: Speak into mic → Gemini responds → audio plays back through speakers. Interruption stops playback. Verify round-trip works with a simple Hello test.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Track queue depth, playback latency, interruption flush counts, and resample ratios in structured logs so audio regressions can be diagnosed quickly.
- Keep playback buffering deterministic enough that transcript fallback and connection-state UI can explain what happened when audio output fails.

title: BE-7: Drag Detection → Context Injection + Edge Creation
description:
Turn user drag intent into deterministic structure per spec §8.4, but do not rely on the model to perform an undefined edge-creation action.

- Frontend sends drag-end event with card ID, origin, destination, and current viewport context.
- Backend computes the nearest card and distance.
- If distance < 80px (proximity threshold):
  - Create the dependency edge immediately on the backend if an equivalent edge does not already exist.
  - Inject [CANVAS_UPDATE] context per spec §8.4 so the model can verbally interpret the relationship signal.
  - Use turnComplete: true so the agent can acknowledge the spatial intent.
- If distance >= 80px: inject a simple position update with turnComplete: false and persist the new position without creating an edge.
- Edge creation must be idempotent and deduplicated.
- Notify the frontend so the SVG arrow appears immediately, independent of whether the model speaks.
- Record the drag/edge event for replay and debugging.

Acceptance: Drag card A near card B (within 80px). The backend persists exactly one dependency edge, injects relationship context, and the SVG edge appears immediately. Drag card to empty space: silent position update, no edge. Repeating the same proximity gesture does not create duplicate edges.
acceptance_criteria: Drag card A near card B (within 80px). The backend persists exactly one dependency edge, injects relationship context, and the SVG edge appears immediately. Drag card to empty space: silent position update, no edge. Repeating the same proximity gesture does not create duplicate edges.

title: CU-3: Ghost Card Animation

- After 300ms: transition to solid state
- Each card has independent 300ms timer (not synchronized across cards)

acceptance_criteria: Creating a card shows ghost state for 300ms then smoothly transitions to solid. Multiple cards created in rapid succession each have their own independent animation.

title: EX-1: Google Calendar Integration
description:
Implement create_calendar_event tool per spec §7.4.

Note: Requires human checkpoint (STEP 5 in pre-swarm DAG) for OAuth credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Gemini calls create_calendar_event → event appears in Google Calendar → confirmation card appears on canvas.

- Handle token refresh explicitly and log refresh failures distinctly from event-insert failures.

title: CU-11: Light/Dark Theme Toggle
description:
Implement the deferred theme-switching feature from spec §4.3 / §11 without degrading the strong dark-theme default used in the demo.

- Persist the selected theme at the workspace level so reloads preserve the user’s choice.
- Ensure cards, edges, replay UI, transcript panel, controls, and status indicators all honor the active theme tokens.
- Preserve the dark theme as the default demo aesthetic while making light mode polished rather than incidental.
- Keep contrast and readability high in both themes.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Users can switch between dark and light themes at runtime.
2. The selected theme persists across reloads for the same workspace.
3. Core canvas surfaces and overlays restyle consistently in both themes.
4. No demo-critical UI becomes unreadable in either theme.
5. Theme changes are replay-safe and do not corrupt artifact state.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Users can switch between dark and light themes at runtime.

- Tag chips rendered below body
- Card dimensions: ~200-280px wide, height auto

- data-id attribute for DOM manipulation by force layout

acceptance_criteria: Render a card with mock data at (100, 100). Card shows title, body, tags, correct status color. Card is draggable.

title: CU-8: Connection Status Indicator
description:
WebSocket connection state indicator per spec §5.5.

- States: Connecting (pulsing amber), Connected (green), Reconnecting (amber), Error (red with message)
- Driven by WebSocket connection state

Acceptance: Indicator shows correct state as WebSocket connects, disconnects, and reconnects.
acceptance_criteria: Indicator shows correct state as WebSocket connects, disconnects, and reconnects.

title: EX-3: Research Job Integration
description:
Wire research into the Live API session per spec §7.3 background execution flow.
- start_research_job tool handler:
  1. Create job node artifact on canvas (status: running, pulsing animation)
  2. Return { job_id, artifact_id, status: started } to Gemini immediately (blocking tool — model waits)
  3. After tool response, kick off async research via EX-2
  4. When results return, inject sendClientContent with the [SYSTEM] research results prompt (spec §7.3 step 3)
  5. The prompt instructs agent to create individual cards for each finding (research cascading)
  6. Update job node status to complete

Acceptance: Say research compliance requirements → job node appears with pulse → research runs → results inject → agent announces findings and creates multiple cards → cards enter force simulation.
acceptance_criteria: Say research compliance requirements → job node appears with pulse → research runs → results inject → agent announces findings and creates multiple cards → cards enter force simulation.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Persist both the visible job-node artifact and a backing Job record so status transitions are queryable and replayable.
- Handle the failure path explicitly: failed research should update the job node, log the reason, and avoid leaving the canvas in a permanent running state.
- Result cards should be created near the job node first and then be allowed to join semantic force layout rather than remaining visually stranded.

title: CU-1: Canvas Container
description:
Create the pannable, zoomable canvas per spec §11.
- Canvas.tsx component
- Absolutely-positioned container with CSS transforms for pan/zoom
- Mouse drag to pan, scroll wheel to zoom
- Coordinate system: (0,0) at viewport center, spec §8.2
- Dark theme default (background color, subtle grid optional)
- Render children (artifact cards) at their (x, y) positions

Acceptance: Empty canvas renders. Can pan and zoom. Coordinate system verified: a div at (0,0) appears at viewport center.
acceptance_criteria: Empty canvas renders. Can pan and zoom. Coordinate system verified: a div at (0,0) appears at viewport center.

title: CU-10: Research Job Node Component
description:
Build the dedicated research job artifact UI described in spec §§7.3, 11.1, and 13.2 Shot 3/4 so research work is visible, legible, and trustworthy to users.

FILES:
- components/JobNode.tsx
- supporting styles in the existing canvas/card styling layer

SCOPE:
- Render a distinct job-node artifact for type:"job" that is visually different from normal cards.
- Status variants: running, complete, failed.
- Running state: pulsing animation, active indicator, concise subtitle such as "Researching...".
- Complete state: pulse stops, completion badge appears, summary/count metadata can render.
- Failed state: error styling and short failure message.
- Render title/topic, current status, timestamps or duration metadata when available, and finding/source counts after completion.
- Expose stable DOM hooks (data-id, data-status, data-testid) so force-layout code, replay, and e2e tests can target the node reliably.
- Respect the shared canvas coordinate system and drag model so the node can participate in layout/replay like other artifacts.
- Support ghost-to-solid behavior when the node is first created, but keep the running pulse active after solidification until the job completes.
- Ensure the component reads cleanly at demo zoom levels and remains visually distinct from synthesis cards and calendar confirmation cards.

USER-FOCUSED BEHAVIOR:
- Users should be able to tell at a glance that background work has started, is still active, and has completed.
- The component should communicate progress without requiring audio.
- Failure should be visible on-canvas instead of disappearing into logs.
acceptance_criteria:
1. JobNode.tsx renders running, complete, and failed variants for type: job artifacts.
2. Running nodes visibly pulse after ghost solidification until completion.
3. Complete and failed transitions update styles in place without remounting the artifact.
4. The node can be positioned, dragged, replayed, and targeted by SVG edges like any other artifact.
5. Stable selectors exist for unit/e2e tests and DOM-based layout logic.
6. Mock rendering demonstrates legible UI at normal canvas zoom.

title: AU-2: Backend WebSocket Server
description:
Create the Bun-based WebSocket server that proxies between browser and Gemini. Per spec §5.1.
- Express or raw HTTP server in app/api/ or standalone server file
- WebSocket upgrade handler (use ws library)
- Accept browser connections
- Define explicit browser/backend message types: health, audio_in, audio_out, transcript, tool_result, canvas_update, connection_state, error, and heartbeat/ping
- Health check endpoint
- Environment variable loading from .env.local
acceptance_criteria:
1. The server starts under `bun run dev`, not npm.
2. A browser client can connect, exchange heartbeat/ping traffic, and send/receive typed or audio session messages.
3. Health checks and connection_state/error messages work end-to-end.
4. Socket lifecycle logs include session/workspace identifiers and reconnect-relevant metadata.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Correct the runtime expectation to Bun only: acceptance should be verified with bun run dev, never npm.
- Define an explicit message schema for browser/backend traffic: health, audio_in, audio_out, transcript, tool_result, canvas_update, connection_state, error, and heartbeat/ping.
- Include heartbeat/reconnect semantics so the connection-status UI can be driven from real backend state instead of guesswork.
- Log socket lifecycle events with session/workspace identifiers.

title: HC-1: OAuth Credentials Human Checkpoint
description:
HUMAN-ONLY TASK: Create Google Cloud OAuth credentials for Calendar integration.

This bead BLOCKS the Calendar integration TEST (IN-5) but does NOT block Calendar CODE (EX-1).
The code can be written without credentials; testing requires them.

STEPS:
1. Go to Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials
2. Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application type)
3. Set redirect URI to match GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI in .env
4. Enable Google Calendar API in the project
5. Authorize a test Google account:
   - Run a one-time OAuth consent flow
   - Capture the refresh token
6. Store credentials in .env.local:
   GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<client_id>
   GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<client_secret>
   GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback
   GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN=<refresh_token>

IMPORTANT (spec §5.2, AGENTS.md Rule 5):
- No agent can create these credentials — they require browser-based Google Account login
- The code in EX-1 should accept these from environment variables
- Pre-authorize a test account so the demo works without OAuth prompts
- For Cloud Run deployment, set these as env vars in the deploy command

NOTES:
- The refresh token must be for a Google account that has a Google Calendar
- Test by creating a test event after authorization
- Calendar API quota: 1,000,000 queries/day (more than enough)

title: BE-13: group_artifacts Tool + Group Rendering
description:
Capture the spec’s deferred grouping feature in a single bead so it is not lost from the roadmap.

SCOPE:

- Create a group artifact with title, member artifact IDs, workspace ownership, and metadata for membership.
- Render a visible group treatment on the canvas so grouping is more than a backend-only data operation.
- Group membership should influence semantic layout strength and any future topology analysis.
- The grouped result must remain replayable and persist cleanly in Firestore.
- Avoid blocking the initial demo build; this is an upgrade path, not part of the effective P0 path.

USER VALUE:
- Users can compress a busy canvas into named structures once enough artifacts exist.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Calling group_artifacts with valid member IDs creates a persisted group artifact.
2. Group members render with a visible shared grouping treatment or container.
3. Group membership persists through reload and replay.
4. Semantic layout uses group membership as a stronger attraction signal.
5. Invalid or duplicate member IDs are rejected with clear error output and logs.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Calling group_artifacts with valid member IDs creates a persisted group artifact.

title: CU-12: Multi-View Transformations
description:
Preserve the spec’s P2 multi-view canvas transformations so the roadmap still includes alternate spatial lenses beyond the default semantic layout.

SCOPE:
- Implement the documented views: timeline view, priority view, dependency view, and big-picture/minimap view.
- Views should animate with intentional 500ms transitions rather than hard cuts.
- Transformations must preserve artifact identity so replay/history/tool references still map to the same objects.
- Dependency view should emphasize relationships without destroying the underlying workspace state.
- The system may trigger view changes through voice/tooling or explicit UI controls, but the implementation must define one reliable path.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Each documented view can be activated and exited cleanly.
2. Switching views does not lose artifact positions/state from the primary canvas model.
3. Animations remain smooth and readable at demo scale.
4. Dependency view exposes relationships more clearly than the default mode.
5. Logs/telemetry record active view changes for debugging and replay context.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each documented view can be activated and exited cleanly.

title: BE-12: On-Demand Canvas Snapshot Grounding
description:
Implement the P1 visual-grounding path from spec §8.5 so spatial references can be resolved with a single screenshot instead of continuous video.

SCOPE:
- Add a frontend capture path that can snapshot the current canvas viewport on demand.
- Send the captured image to the backend only when a spatial reference or ambiguous visual descriptor justifies it.
- Inject the image into the live session via clientContent, not realtimeInput, so the app avoids the 2-minute audio-video session limit.
- Include enough metadata with the image injection to relate the screenshot to the current viewport center, zoom, and visible artifacts.
- Downscale/compress images to a predictable budget suitable for repeated use in a long session.
- Debounce repeated requests so multiple near-identical screenshots are not injected back-to-back.
- Add structured logging around capture timing, image size, and injection success/failure.

USER VALUE:
- References like "that red card" or "the dense cluster on the right" become more reliable without paying the cost of a full video session.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. A triggered spatial-reference flow can request and receive a fresh canvas screenshot.
2. The screenshot is injected via clientContent with the matching viewport metadata.
3. No continuous video stream is introduced.
4. Repeated identical requests are debounced.
5. Logs record capture size, capture duration, and injection outcome.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A triggered spatial-reference flow can request and receive a fresh canvas screenshot.

title: BE-11: Dynamic System Instruction Mutation
description:
Implement the P1 phase-based prompt mutation path from spec §5.2.1 so the agent’s behavior can evolve as the canvas matures without inflating the base system prompt.

SCOPE:
- Maintain explicit phase state: divergent (0-4 cards), convergent (5-9 cards), critical (10+ cards).
- Store the phase prompts directly in code with the behavior text from the spec, not just threshold constants.
- Use session.sendClientContent() with role: system to send phase updates.
- Transitions are one-way per session and fire only once when thresholds are crossed.
- Gate the whole feature behind a feature flag so the reliable baseline build can ship without it.
- Record structured logs whenever a phase transition is attempted, succeeds, is skipped, or is disabled.
- If the SDK/session rejects system-role client content, fail gracefully and keep the session alive.

WHY THIS EXISTS:
- It preserves the full roadmap from the spec.
- It keeps the base prompt concise while still allowing richer late-session synthesis/refinement behavior.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Crossing 5 artifacts emits exactly one convergent-phase system update.
2. Crossing 10 artifacts emits exactly one critical-phase system update.
3. Repeated pauses or reconnects do not spam duplicate phase updates.
4. Feature-flag-off mode leaves the baseline behavior unchanged.
5. Logs show threshold, prior phase, new phase, and delivery result for every transition attempt.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Crossing 5 artifacts emits exactly one convergent-phase system update.

title: CU-13: Thinking Budget Indicator
description:
Preserve the spec’s deferred thinking-budget visualization as a subtle, non-chatty UI feature.

SCOPE:
- Add a lightweight on-canvas indicator that shows when the model is actively reasoning and then clears quickly.
- The indicator must feel like ambient system state, not a verbose chain-of-thought dump.
- Keep the visual treatment brief and restrained so it supports trust without distracting from the canvas itself.
- Tie the signal to actual model/session events where possible, not arbitrary timers.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Users can tell when the agent is actively processing a complex turn.
2. The indicator never exposes chain-of-thought text.
3. The UI disappears promptly after the reasoning window ends.
4. The indicator does not conflict with transcript, replay, or connection-status UI.
5. Logging/telemetry can confirm when and why the indicator was shown.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Users can tell when the agent is actively processing a complex turn.

title: IN-8: Connection Recovery + Session Resumption
description:
Implement the recovery path described across spec §§5.2, 5.5, and 10.2 so transient socket/API failures degrade visibly and resume safely.

SCOPE:
- Detect browser↔backend disconnects, backend↔Gemini session drops, and interrupted playback states from real transport/session signals.
- Drive the user-visible connection lifecycle through Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting, and Error using backend-authored events.
- Attempt Live API session resumption and browser WebSocket reconnection with bounded retry/backoff behavior.
- Preserve existing canvas state and transcript history while reconnecting so the UI never collapses to an empty shell during a transient failure.
- Prevent duplicate tool execution, duplicate artifact creation, and stale audio replay when a turn is resumed or replayed after reconnect.
- Emit structured logs for reconnect attempts, resume tokens/session IDs, elapsed timings, audio-queue flushes, and hard-failure reasons.

USER VALUE:
- Users can see that the system is recovering instead of guessing whether it silently died.
- A brief network hiccup does not destroy trust in the session.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A simulated connection loss moves the UI into Reconnecting promptly and returns it to Connected after a successful resume.
2. If recovery fails, the UI lands in the red Error state with the specified user-facing guidance while leaving the current canvas/transcript visible.
3. Resume/reconnect paths do not duplicate cards, edges, tool side effects, or audio playback.
4. Transcript history and canvas state continue from the pre-drop state after recovery.
5. Logs record reconnect attempt count, timing, resume outcome, and unrecoverable failure reason.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- This is a user-trust bead, not just resilience plumbing; the visible connection-state story must be demo-ready before deployment and smoke testing.
- Coordinate backend heartbeat, session-resumption support, audio-queue clearing, and connection-indicator UI so they behave as one coherent recovery system.
- Keep manual refresh as the last resort, not the first-line recovery path.

title: BE-9: Synthesis Trigger
description:
After the canvas reaches synthesis-worthy density, inject a grounded synthesis prompt only when the layout and silence conditions make it useful. This bead should make synthesis reliable, not hopeful.

- Track artifact count, last-audio-input timestamp, and the most recent settled topology signature.
- When count >= 5, force layout is settled (or freeze timeout elapsed), and the user has been silent for 2+ seconds:
  - Inject the [SYSTEM] synthesis prompt from spec §11.1.1 via sendClientContent.
  - Ensure the prompt is sent only once per meaningful synthesis window unless the canvas has materially changed.
- Gate re-triggering with both a cooldown and a structural-change check so the user is not spammed with repeated synthesis attempts.
- Coordinate with topology analysis so the synthesis prompt always follows the most current spatial structure.
- Emit structured logs for threshold checks, silence checks, topology state, prompt delivery, and skipped/debounced cases.

Acceptance: After creating 5+ cards, waiting for layout settle, and pausing, exactly one synthesis prompt is injected for that canvas state. The prompt is not re-sent on repeated pauses unless the artifact/topology state changes materially.
acceptance_criteria: After creating 5+ cards, waiting for layout settle, and pausing, exactly one synthesis prompt is injected for that canvas state. The prompt is not re-sent on repeated pauses unless the artifact/topology state changes materially.

title: IN-3: Drag + Edge Integration
description:
Wire drag intent all the way through to immediate structural feedback.

- User drags a card.
- Frontend emits the drag event.
- Backend applies the deterministic edge-creation/idempotency rules from BE-7.
- Frontend renders the new SVG arrow immediately.
- Force layout incorporates the resulting relationship on the next reheat/settle cycle.
- The model may verbally acknowledge the relationship, but the visible structural result must not depend on speech generation.

Acceptance: Drag card A near card B. Exactly one edge appears immediately, force layout treats the cards as linked, and the agent can optionally acknowledge the connection. Repeating the gesture does not create duplicate edges.
acceptance_criteria: Drag card A near card B. Exactly one edge appears immediately, force layout treats the cards as linked, and the agent can optionally acknowledge the connection. Repeating the gesture does not create duplicate edges.

title: BE-2: Tool Executor Framework
description:
Receive tool calls from Gemini, validate, execute, return results. Per spec §7.1.
- When Gemini emits a toolCall event, parse the function name and arguments
- Route to the appropriate handler (create_card, move_artifact, etc.)
- Validate required parameters
- Execute handler (writes to Firestore)
- Return result object directly to SDK — do NOT JSON.stringify() (spec §7.1 critical note)
- Send tool response back to Live API session via session.sendToolResponse()
- Notify frontend of canvas change via WebSocket
- Record state snapshot for replay (spec §11.3)

Acceptance: When Gemini calls create_card, the executor: validates args, writes to Firestore, returns result to Gemini, notifies frontend. Tool response includes artifact_id and position.
acceptance_criteria: When Gemini calls create_card, the executor: validates args, writes to Firestore, returns result to Gemini, notifies frontend. Tool response includes artifact_id and position.
notes:
PLAN REVISION:
- Keep the executor framework modular: handler execution, frontend notification, canvas-state injection, and snapshot recording should be separable hooks/services so later upgrade beads do not have to rewrite the core dispatch loop.
- Treat all initial canvas/action tools as blocking; do not emit FunctionResponseScheduling from the baseline executor.
- Add structured logging for tool-call receipt, validation outcome, handler latency, Firestore write latency, frontend notification payloads, and failure paths.
